items.title: UN envoy intervenes in Woodside gas plant challenge

children.content: The federal government's extension of Woodside's North West Shelf gas project on the Burrup Peninsula by another 40 years could violate human rights, an independent United Nations expert warns.

gallery.altText: A woman smiles while giving an address at a podium.
A gas flame burns as the sun sets over Woodside's Karratha processing plant.

thumbnail.altText: A woman smiles while giving an address at a podium.
A gas flame burns as the sun sets over Woodside's Karratha processing plant.

items.title: Yacht intercepted by police off Exmouth allegedly carrying outlaw gang member

children.content: WA Police and Australian Federal Police intercepted a yacht off the coast of Exmouth in northern WA which allegedly had a Queensland fugitive on board.

image.altText: A white yacht on left and inflatable boat on right both on ocean

items.title: Concerns groundwater use by mining companies leaving towns 'vulnerable'

children.content: As a major miner seeks approval to extend its magnetite ore operations in regional Western Australia to 2048, shires are increasingly concerned about ensuring there's enough groundwater to go around.

image.altText: A tank protests a mining water licence application

items.title: The choir helping people turn their lives around

children.content: In an unassuming building in Perth's north, the Second Chance Choir is bringing people together through music to help them put aside years of trauma, violence and substance abuse.

gallery.altText: Close-up of a group of people singing in a choir.
A close up image of a microphone

thumbnail.altText: Close-up of a group of people singing in a choir.
A close up image of a microphone

items.title: Murder victim tied up with plastic wrap, stomped in meth-fuelled burglary

children.content: Two men are convicted of killing 68-year-old Raymond Smith in his own home in Western Australia's South West during a meth-addled burglary for drugs.

image.altText: A man with glasses and his hair in a ponytail looks away from the camera.

items.title: Water Corp says maintenance of 54,000km of pipes a challenge amid complaints

children.content: For two years, residents in one of the driest parts of Western Australia have watched thousands of litres of water leaking from a Water Corp-owned tank.

image.altText: Water surrounds the leaking tank

items.title: Teen's 5-day wait in WA emergency department sparks official apology

children.content: The WA health minister and director of the state's country health service have unreservedly apologised to a 17-year-old left in an emergency room for 116 hours waiting for specialised mental health care.

image.altText: Madi sits on the foreshore in her hometown of Busselton

items.title: Gold reserves grow at Kalgoorlie's Super Pit

children.content: A big exploration push by Australia's biggest gold miner, Northern Star Resources, has grown the reserves at the Super Pit by 54 per cent in the past six years.

image.altText: A mining truck carrying gold-bearing ore.

items.title: Australia joins exclusive club granting access to advanced AI

children.content: Fresh access to AI deemed too dangerous for public release has ramped up pressure on the government to respond to possible risks.

articles.title: Economic report says 82 occupations have been 'AI-disrupted'

children.content: A new economic report by consultancy firm Deloitte says the AI "winners" will be organisations that combine human and machine strengths, with recruiters saying human judgement will still be key.

image.altText: A woman in a white shirt and long hair looks at camera.

articles.title: What's got the pope and most Australians worried? This hyped new technology

children.content: Most Australians appear to distrust and dislike artificial intelligence despite using technology more than people in other countries.

image.altText: A close up of Pope Leo.

gallery.altText: Anthropic logo is seen in this photo

thumbnail.altText: Anthropic logo is seen in this photo

items.title: ASX falls on renewed Middle East tensions

children.content: The ASX has fallen by nearly 1 per cent following Wall Street slipping overnight as hopes for a swift end to the war in Iran faded again. Follow the latest on our live markets blog.

image.altText: An ASX board shot by John Gunn in Deccember 2022.

items.title: Docker O'Driscoll says mental health admission 'best thing' he's done

children.content: Fremantle Dockers midfielder Nathan O'Driscoll says he has received "nothing but love" after posting on social media about his struggles with anxiety and how he contemplated taking his own life.

image.altText: Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll celebrates a goal with a shout in front of fans at Perth Stadium, in Fremantle's white jersey.

items.title: Medical clinics closing down 'like dominoes' with new bulk-billing model

children.content: Residents in the Midwest town of Kalbarri are facing a minimum two-hour round trip to see a doctor, with the local GP announcing it will close later this month.

gallery.altText: A sign that reads Kalbarri Doctors Surgery is in the distance boarderd by leaves and trees.
Aerial view over rivermouth and foreshore lined with trees
Image of Monica wearing a t shirt and her hair tied back. She stands in front of the ocean and looks concerned at camera.

thumbnail.altText: A sign that reads Kalbarri Doctors Surgery is in the distance boarderd by leaves and trees.
Aerial view over rivermouth and foreshore lined with trees

items.title: Sportsbet's owner walks through 'wide open gate' in gambling laws

children.content: One of the world's biggest gambling companies is breaking into the 'rewards club' scene, raising the alarm among anti-gambling advocates.

image.altText: Two AFL players on a field, a sportsbet logo in the background.

items.title: WHO says Ebola outbreak may have started as early as January

children.content: Militant attacks have hampered efforts to combat the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda.

image.altText: A person wearing full-body and face PPE including a face mask standing in a medical tent.

items.title: 'A huge gamble': How design shows choose their projects

children.content: A planned 12-month build that's still underway three years later is the running joke of design shows. But what happens when you're a TV producer trying to make a series of it?

image.altText: A camera films an old building with scaffolding around it.

items.title: How to move forward from estrangement

children.content: Experts say healing might involve reducing emotional distress and gaining clarity from the situation.

image.altText: Two people sitting on a park bench with their backs to the camera

items.title: Strongest storm in half a century caps off your top WA weather photos for May

children.content: Bursts of sunshine, brief spells of rain and long stretches of dry skies across May left many West Australians wondering when winter would finally arrive — until it did with a bang. Take a look back in pictures.

gallery.altText: A silhouette of teenage girls playing volleyball on the beach at sunset
Mist settles over a trees in an image with blue hues
Waves crash over Sugarloaf Rock with whitewater spraying into the air.

thumbnail.altText: A silhouette of teenage girls playing volleyball on the beach at sunset
Mist settles over a trees in an image with blue hues
Waves crash over Sugarloaf Rock with whitewater spraying into the air.

items.title: Quiz: Can you recognise these 10 classic Australian album covers?

children.content: Take a closer look at some of the biggest albums in Australian music and tell us what you see.

image.altText: Australian singer-songwriter Missy Higgins sits in a blue satin dress, arms folded, against black backdrop

items.title: Why some home owners are at risk of slipping into negative equity

children.content: A 46-year-old former "forever renter" believes her house is now worth less than what she paid for it and she is at risk of slipping into negative equity, which would mean her Marrickville home could be worth less than what she owes the bank.

image.altText: A woman holding up a cup of coffee

items.title: Cannabis driving limit change to give 'freedom' to medicinal users in NSW

children.content: The Labor government plans to introduce a legal limit on the concentration of THC a driver can have in their system if they have a prescription.

image.altText: Police officers testing drivers for cannabis near Nimbin

items.title: Heatwaves are becoming so extreme, they're 'breaking the biology' of plants

children.content: Record-breaking heatwaves decimated crops in Australia this year and served as a "real wake-up call" for the urgent need to develop more heat-resistant crops and research into food security.

image.altText: a generic photo of a wheat crop

items.title: Pilot 'unsure' which button was pressed before plane crashed in Broome

children.content: Australia's transport safety watchdog is investigating whether a pilot inadvertently turned a plane's engine off before it crashed in WA's north earlier this year, with five passengers and two pilots on board.

gallery.altText: A cessna plane wreckage with writing overtop of picture
A damaged cessna plane steering wheel

thumbnail.altText: A cessna plane wreckage with writing overtop of picture
A damaged cessna plane steering wheel

items.title: Call to increase no-fish, no-drill zones as marine park review looms

children.content: Australia's dive industry has backed calls to expand no-fishing and no-drilling zones across the nation's oceans ahead of a federal review of marine parks.

image.altText: A diver swims close to the ocean floor

items.title: 'Infuriating': Aboriginal voters turned away, told to line up again on SA election day

children.content: South Australia's Electoral Commission was aware that forcing First Nations voters to queue twice for the Voice election would be perceived as "discriminatory behaviour". Despite that, voters say it still happened and, in some cases, they were turned away.

image.altText: A woman wearing a maroon shirt and checked cardigan looks over her right shoulder.

items.title: Copper thieves are more brazen and taking exceptional risks, demolition experts say

children.content: Demolition sites are being stripped for copper at an escalating rate, according to a Perth demolition contractor, risking electrocution and asbestos exposure to sell the scrap metal on the black market.

image.altText: Copper wiring bundles into circles

items.title: 'Extraordinary career': Hundreds farewell veteran MP at state funeral

children.content: Hundreds of mourners farewell WA's longest-serving parliamentarian, war hero Bill Grayden, at his state funeral in Perth.

image.altText: A portrait of Bill Grayden on a table during a state funeral.

items.title: Woman credits 'divine intervention' after tree hits car in huge WA storm

children.content: A mother shares her incredible tale of survival after a tree falls on her car in a freak accident on a South West highway during WA's strongest storm in 49 years.

articles.title: Strongest storm system in 49 years tears through Perth, southern WA

children.content: Tens of thousands of homes are left without power and the SES receives hundreds of calls for help, with the roofs torn off two apartment buildings, as WA's southern half is hammered by more severe weather.

image.altText: A badly damaged apartment block with its roof torn off and metal debris.

gallery.altText: A car with significant damage parked on the side of the road at night
A damaged car on the side of a highway.

thumbnail.altText: A car with significant damage parked on the side of the road at night
A damaged car on the side of a highway.
